steps.title: Compare the discoveries
steps.content: The goal is to compare Caldwell's discovery with Halpern's. Caldwell discovered mobile DNA segments that can change position in a genome. Halpern discovered how phage DNA enters a bacterial host cell and becomes part of the host genome. The comparison should connect those two forms of DNA movement.

steps.title: Remove mismatched claims
steps.content: Choice B reverses the discoveries, assigning phage DNA to Caldwell and mobile genome segments to Halpern. Choice C incorrectly makes both discoveries about phage DNA and the lysogenic cycle. Choice D wrongly says Halpern explained how transposable elements move.

steps.title: Choose the shared feature
steps.content: The remaining choice accurately says Caldwell's work involved moving DNA segments and Halpern's work involved phage DNA entering and incorporating into a host cell's genome. The answer is A.
